game_name: Dune II: The Building of a Dynasty

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns to place structures on rocky terrain only, build concrete foundations for durability
Identify orange spice fields, manage harvester routes, convert spice to credits via refinery
Convert harvested spice into credits for unit construction funding
Defend base from initial enemy incursions, build basic infantry and light vehicles
Scout terrain to remove permanent fog of war, discover enemy positions and spice locations
Learn sandworm moves only in sandy areas, avoid with units, use rocky terrain as barrier
Recognize terrain bumps as concealed spice, trigger blooms with fire/contact, manage unit loss risk
Manage structure decay over time; concrete slabs reduce repair costs but don't prevent it entirely
Progress unlocks faction-specific units: Harkonnen Devastator, Atreides Sonic Tank, Ordos Deviator
Harkonnen slower/heavier, Atreides balanced, Ordos faster/specialized; trikes vs. Quad variants
Shift from passive harvesting defense to offensive multi-unit assaults on enemy strongholds
Capture enemy Factory to build rival Houses' specialized units; strategic unit composition
Three Houses competing for control; enemy houses begin coordinated pressure campaigns
Rival Houses form temporary alliance; Sardaukar elite forces enter as final challenge

typical_relief_triggers: Establishing secure base perimeter with defensive structures
Advancing to next territory via world map (mission transition)
typical_tension_triggers: Sandworm appearance in active spice zones
Rival House offensive coordinated across multiple fronts
Resource starvation from blocked harvesting routes
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Gradual escalation from economic management (missions 1-2) through territorial conquest (missions 3-8) to final overwhelming multi-faction assault (mission 9). Early game emphasizes resource mastery; mid-game introduces military complexity via faction units; late game forces simultaneous resource gathering and defensive/offensive operations.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Spice field density (darker orange = higher concentration); concealed spice blooms as terrain anomalies
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Rocky terrain islands act as safe construction zones; sandworms implicitly confined to sand; concealed spice blooms reward exploration with risk

difficulty_curve.early_game: Learning curve: establish base economics, defend against light raids, master terrain constraints (rocky construction zones, sandworm avoidance). Missions 1-2 focus on uncontested spice harvesting.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalating pressure: enemy bases appear; player must balance harvesting with offensive unit construction. New faction-specific units unlock, forcing unit composition decisions. Missions 3-6 require destroying first enemy strongholds.
difficulty_curve.late_game: Overwhelming complexity: three-way territorial conflict; resource pressure intensifies; Sardaukar elite forces arrive. Missions 7-9 require simultaneous defense and multi-front offense. Final mission: allied enemy houses + Sardaukar = hardest challenge.
notable_spikes: Mission 3: First enemy base destruction objective (shift from passive to active warfare)
Mission 7+: Multi-front territorial conflicts as rival Houses coordinate pressure
Final mission: Two allied enemy Houses + Sardaukar elite forces (resource and military overwhelming force)

notable_level_patterns.description: Player establishes base on unoccupied territory, harvests spice uncontested, defends against initial light raids. Emphasizes resource management and terrain mastery.
Player selects next territory from world map (2-3 options); territory choice determines enemy House faced. Mission requires destroying enemy base while maintaining harvesting.
Dominant player's House faces temporary alliance of two rival Houses + Sardaukar elite. Highest resource pressure, simultaneous multi-directional threats.
Higher missions unlock faction-specific units and super weapons. Completing mission N authorizes technology unavailable in mission N-1. Forces player to adapt strategy per faction.
